description:
You are a performance-engineer who has just finished collecting
    core-dump samples for a badly behaving program.  
    All dumps live in  

        /home/user/app_dumps  

    and every file in that directory that ends in “.core” must now be
    compressed and accounted for.

    Your goals:

    1. Use a single Unix pipeline that is based **only** on
       “find … | xargs …” (you may add flags such as -0 or -print0
       and any gzip options you like) to gzip-compress every
       “*.core” file located directly inside
       /home/user/app_dumps.  
       • The pipeline has to delete the original .core
         files automatically (that is the normal behaviour
         of gzip – you do **not** need to delete them manually).  
       • After successful completion there must be **exactly three**
         “*.core.gz” files in /home/user/app_dumps and **no**
         plain “*.core” files left.

    2. When the compression step is finished, create a CSV log file

        /home/user/app_dumps/compression.log  

       whose contents will be checked by an automated grader.

       Required format of compression.log:
       • UTF-8 text, Unix line endings.  
       • No header row.  
       • One line per core-dump, sorted alphabetically
         (byte-wise `LC_ALL=C` sort order) by the **file name** that
         appears in the first column.  
       • Each line has three comma-separated columns with **no
         spaces at all**:
           1. original file name, including the “.core” extension,
              but **without** any leading path.
           2. the size of the original .core file in bytes
              (`stat -c%s` or `wc -c` after reading the file).  
           3. the size of the newly created .core.gz file in bytes,
              again as a plain integer.

       Strict example (values here are only illustrations – yours
       will differ):

           crash1.core,15728640,248750
           crash2.core,7340032,197451
           crash3.core,8192,1430

       • Make sure there are exactly three lines – one for each
         compressed dump – and that every column is an integer.

    Verification checklist (the grader will look for all of this):
    • No “*.core” files remain, only “*.core.gz” plus the log.  
    • compression.log exists at
      /home/user/app_dumps/compression.log.  
    • Log file contains three correctly ordered, correctly formatted
      lines.  
    • Each first-column file name matches a corresponding “.core.gz”
      file (same base name).  
    • Second column equals the byte size of the original dump before
      compression.  
    • Third column equals the byte size of the corresponding
      .core.gz file that currently exists on disk.

    Work entirely in the terminal – no GUI tools – and rely on
    find + xargs for the batch operation.
truth:
<!--  Files that exist before the agent starts  -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/app_dumps/crash1.core">CORE_DUMP_1
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/app_dumps/crash2.core">CORE_DUMP_2
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/app_dumps/crash3.core">CORRUPTED_CORE_DUMP
</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--  Exact byte sizes of the original dumps (used by tests)  -->
    <original_sizes>
        <crash1>12</crash1>   <!-- "CORE_DUMP_1\n" -->
        <crash2>12</crash2>   <!-- "CORE_DUMP_2\n" -->
        <crash3>20</crash3>   <!-- "CORRUPTED_CORE_DUMP\n" -->
    </original_sizes>

    <!--  After the agent finishes the following must hold  -->
    <expected_state>
        <!-- 1. No plain .core files -->
        <!-- 2. These gzip files must exist; their sizes will be recorded dynamically -->
        <gz_files>
            /home/user/app_dumps/crash1.core.gz
            /home/user/app_dumps/crash2.core.gz
            /home/user/app_dumps/crash3.core.gz
        </gz_files>

        <!-- 3. Log file must exist with three lines, exactly as specified -->
        <log_file_path>/home/user/app_dumps/compression.log</log_file_path>
        <log_line_pattern>^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+\.core,[0-9]+,[0-9]+$</log_line_pattern>
        <log_line_count>3</log_line_count>
        <log_sorted_alphabetically>true</log_sorted_alphabetically>
    </expected_state>
name: task_000000_6c441a85
